profile_basis.summary: Post-pass174 profile routed from rejected exact-order GEMM micro-levers to the top bit-exact non-GEMM row, LU1024.

lever.summary: Add matmul_2d_into and reuse one LU Schur product buffer across blocked panels.

benchmark.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=fmd RCH_WORKERS=fmd RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR RCH_DAEMON_WAIT_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_SECS=180 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=<target> cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'linalg/lu_1024x1024_f64' --sample-size 10 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 3 --noplot

rch_quality_gates: cargo check -p fj-lax --all-targets: passed on fmd
cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets -- -D warnings: passed on fmd
UBS touched files: existing linalg/test panic and indexing inventory reported; UBS internal fmt/clippy/check/test-build sections passed
proof.rustfmt_note: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on linalg.rs still reports pre-existing unrelated formatting drift outside this lever; tensor_contraction.rs formatting is clean.

matmul: matmul_2d_into builds the same MatmulPlan as matmul_2d.
The output buffer is filled with 0.0 before computation, matching the previous freshly allocated zeroed Vec.
Each output row is still owned by one worker and accumulated in ascending k order.
B packing, block-k gating, row chunking, and row-major output layout are unchanged.
lu: Panel order, pivot scan order, strict greater-than tie behavior, and row swap order are unchanged.
The Schur product computes identical bits into a reused buffer.
The final lu[row + q] -= prod[pr + q] loop order is unchanged.
Shape, dtype, error, NaN, signed-zero, and RNG surfaces are unchanged.
